originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'len' is never read

presenceCondition:
(or (and ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH BB_MMU)
    (and ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP (not ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH) BB_MMU)
    (and ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH)
         (not BB_MMU))
    (and ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH (not BB_MMU)))
model:
(define-fun BB_MMU () Bool
  false)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH () Bool
  true)
reasoning: len is assigned at L25 (sprintf) and possibly incremented at L30 (BASIC_AUTH branch) and L36 (if content_gzip), but its final value is never used — printf at L38 uses iobuf directly. This is a genuine dead store. No ERROR comment in file and no existing TRUE record for 95755181b82.c, so this is a new bug not in the VBDB. The PC simplifies to GZIP=T (4 clauses covering all combos of BASIC_AUTH and BB_MMU). GZIP=T is required for compilation since content_gzip (L35) is only declared under #ifdef ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP. Neither BASIC_AUTH nor BB_MMU affect whether len is dead. Correctly constrained.
